headline: Eight engines.
body: A Concert Venue, Hotel, Restaurant, Speakeasy, Ballroom, Spa, Private club, and Market — each programmed to feed the others.  Hotel guests fill the venue.  Venue crowds fill the restaurant.  Club members fill the hotel.

engines.name: Event Venue · 800 Seats
engines.description: The anchor — a converted landmark church. Advised by the operators of the Ryman Auditorium.

engines.name: Wood-Fire & Amaro
engines.description: Chef Joel Valente — three Michelin stars. First floor. Premium without pretension.

engines.name: Speakeasy & Cigar Lounge
engines.description: Basement, with an acoustic breakout stage. Craft cocktails — the late-night destination after shows.

engines.name: Ballroom · 4,000 SF
engines.description: Corporate events, weddings, city programming — weekday revenue.

engines.name: Contrast Therapy Spa
engines.description: Custom sauna and cold plunge, ground floor. Hotel-guest access plus public membership.

engines.name: Private VIP Club
engines.description: Side-stage access and two-way glass overlooking the hall — a members-only room woven into the venue calendar.

engines.name: Breakfast Market
engines.description: The district’s morning anchor — locally sourced, open daily, pulling steady foot traffic onto the block.

engines.name: 80-Key Tapestry Hotel
engines.description: A Hilton Tapestry Collection hotel by Purpose Lodging — steps from the stage.
